text.title: Kernel Tuning: Diagnosis and Verification Examples
text.summary: inotify exhaustion diagnosis, swappiness fix verification, BBR activation check, and WebSocket connection handling examples.

section.title: Diagnosing inotify Exhaustion
section.p: Symptom: Claude Code or VS Code shows "ENOSPC: System limit for number of file watchers reached" even though disk is not full.

example.code:
# Check current limit vs usage sysctl fs.inotify.max_user_watches # fs.inotify.max_user_watches = 65536 ← too low
# Count watches consumed by each process find /proc/*/fd -lname anon_inode:inotify 2>/dev/null \ | cut -d/ -f3 \ | xargs -I{} sh -c 'cat /proc/{}/cmdline 2>/dev/null | tr "\0" " "; echo' \ | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10
# Apply fix immediately (survives until next reboot) echo fs.inotify.max_user_watches=524288 | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/60-agent-tuning.conf sudo sysctl --system
# Verify sysctl fs.inotify.max_user_watches # fs.inotify.max_user_watches = 524288 ← fixed

# Check congestion control sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control # net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control = bbr
# Check qdisc tc qdisc show dev eth0 # qdisc fq 8001: root refcnt 2 ...
# If still showing cubic after applying conf: sudo sysctl --system sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control # Should now show bbr

# Before: aggressive swapping at 60 sysctl vm.swappiness # vm.swappiness = 60
# Apply fix echo "vm.swappiness = 10" | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/60-memory.conf sudo sysctl vm.swappiness=10
# Verify sysctl vm.swappiness # vm.swappiness = 10
# Check current swap usage free -h # Swap: 4.0Gi 0B 4.0Gi ← no active swap with RAM available

# Check all four security baseline values sysctl kernel.kptr_restrict kernel.yama.ptrace_scope kernel.dmesg_restrict net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies # kernel.kptr_restrict = 1 # kernel.yama.ptrace_scope = 1 # kernel.dmesg_restrict = 1 # net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1

# System-level file descriptor usage cat /proc/sys/fs/file-nr # 12480 0 2097152 # ↑ allocated ↑ free from allocated ↑ system max
# Per-process FD count (find high consumers) for pid in /proc/[0-9]*; do count=$(ls "$pid/fd" 2>/dev/null | wc -l) name=$(cat "$pid/comm" 2>/dev/null) echo "$count $name" done | sort -rn | head -10
# Verify PAM limits applied (must be in a new login session) ulimit -n # 65535

section.title: Connection Tracking for WebSocket Services
section.p: nginx WebSocket proxy and RabbitMQ AMQP connections consume conntrack table entries. Monitor usage when serving many concurrent long-lived connections.

example.code:
# Check conntrack usage vs limit sudo conntrack -C 2>/dev/null || cat /proc/sys/net/netfilter/nf_conntrack_count # 8241
sysctl net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max # net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max = 262144
# If count/max ratio > 80%, increase nf_conntrack_max echo "net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max = 524288" | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.d/60-network-perf.conf sudo sysctl net.netfilter.nf_conntrack_max=524288

see-also.link: Automated audit: current vs recommended values
